title: Fire detection or early warning using gases
abstract: A detection device for monitoring an area includes a housing and at least one sensor configured to measure an amount of at least one combustion gas. A controller is operably coupled to the at least one sensor. The controller is configured to determine if a combustion event is present within the area in response to the amount of the at least one combustion gas measured by the at least one sensor and initiate an alarm in response to determining that the combustion event is present within the area.
claims: 1. A detection device for monitoring an area comprising: a chamber-less housing; a plurality of sensors arranged at an exterior of the housing, the plurality of sensors being operable to measure an amount of a respective combustion gas of a plurality of combustion gases; and a controller operably coupled to the plurality of sensors, wherein the controller is configured to: receive a signal from each of the plurality of sensors indicative of an amount of a corresponding combustion gas present within the area; determine, in response to the amount of at least two of the plurality of combustion gases measured by the plurality of sensors, if a combustion event is present within the area and whether the combustion event is a smoldering fire or a flaming fire; and initiate an alarm in response to determining that the combustion event is present within the area.
